items.name: Responsibility Conflation
items.summary: A process element combines independent responsibilities that should evolve, be reviewed, authorized or fail separately.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Control-Flow Integrity Violation
items.summary: Required stages, branches, prerequisites or the authoritative finalization path can be bypassed or inconsistently reached.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Authorization Boundary Violation
items.summary: An approval or authorization is applied beyond the explicitly named decision, action, object or responsibility domain.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Evidence–Reality Mismatch
items.summary: A claim, assessment, implementation state or contract is stronger or more concrete than the authoritative evidence supports.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Status–Evidence Mismatch
items.summary: A status, maturity label or validation outcome exceeds the weakest mandatory evidence or check result.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Incomplete Validation Model
items.summary: Validation checks only a partial quality dimension while implying broader correctness, completeness or readiness.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Traceability and Coverage Loss
items.summary: Confirmed requirements, clauses, decisions or source obligations are lost, merged or left uncovered downstream.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: State Coherence Violation
items.summary: Runtime, artifact or maturity state remains valid after upstream changes or conflicts with dependencies and actual execution.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Provenance and Authority Violation
items.summary: An artifact or decision is produced from non-authoritative, unknown or unrecorded sources, templates or contracts.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Boundary and Ownership Violation
items.summary: Responsibilities, artifacts, packages, execution outputs or ownership domains leak across declared boundaries.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Identity and Uniqueness Model Failure
items.summary: Identity, deduplication or uniqueness rules do not represent the real semantic entity being managed.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.

items.name: Policy Enforcement Gap
items.summary: A declared rule, detector or gate exists but is not wired to execution or does not change runtime behavior when violated.
items.acceptance_test: A new case maps here when it represents the same failure in approach independent of process-specific nouns.
